title: crepis purpurea
intro: Crepis purpurea, also known as the rosy hawk's beard, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is often used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Crepis purpurea is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote health benefits such as soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation.
